what_it_does: An end-to-end acceptance script for the E1 milestone. It sets up a temporary FsStore, builds a tiny constant→uppercase graph, runs it through the scheduler, and then asserts that store round-trips, provenance lineage, memoization, compilation, context variables, generic tools, and bridge messages all behave as expected.
observations: Imports contracts C1–C8 plus runtime.store/scheduler/context-variables and two node types (constant, uppercase).
Defines a global mutable ok flag and a check(label, cond) helper that prints PASS/FAIL.
Uses a hardcoded /tmp/company-e1-store directory, removing it before each run.
Builds a two-node Graph with NodeInstance, Edge, and runs it via scheduler.run.
Verifies provenance lineage walks back from up to src and that src has no inputs.
Re-runs the same graph to verify memoization skips both nodes.
Exercises run_address, NodeType/object_info, compile to ExecNode, context variable resolution, tools registry, and bridge message round-trip.
Returns 0 on full success and 1 on any failure.
standards: Single-file acceptance gate that covers store, scheduler, provenance, memo, and all eight contracts in one executable script.
Explicitly tests that a second scheduler run executes nothing because results are memoized.
Validates that tools are generic verbs and not per-node-type names.
conventions: Uses sys.path.insert to make the parent package importable.
Uses an alias compile_graph for runtime.compile.compile to avoid shadowing the builtin.
Follows a docstring that lists the acceptance assertions and a run command.
Uses Pydantic models for contract validation and isinstance checks for protocol conformance.
concerns: Hardcoded /tmp store path could collide if multiple runs overlap, even though it is removed at startup.
Global ok is mutated from inside check, making state implicit.
Multiple independent assertions are bundled into single cond expressions, so a failure gives less granular feedback.
No use of a test framework; the script exits 0/1 and prints lines.
notes: This is the E1 smoke test: it proves the typed spine, hardened store, scheduler, and C1–C8 contracts can all be exercised together through a tiny dataflow.
questions: What exact shape does scheduler.run return, and what fields besides ran and skipped can it contain?
What interfaces do the constant and uppercase node-type modules expose?
What does store.lineage return, and how is provenance stored for source nodes?
What is the Resolver Protocol that FsStore is checked against?
How are tools.TOOLS keyed and what does each entry require?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it runs the E1 acceptance checks for the store, provenance, memoization, and each contract C1–C8.
intention_signals: The file is trying to demonstrate that the E1 stage is integration-ready by wiring the store, scheduler, a minimal graph, and every defined contract into one passing run.
novelty: It treats provenance lineage and memoization skip behavior as first-class acceptance criteria rather than just unit-testing individual functions.
contribution: Provides a single executable acceptance gate that exercises store round-trips, scheduler execution, provenance lineage, memoization, and contract/model sanity checks in one place.
summary_for_embedding: An end-to-end acceptance test script for the E1 stage that exercises the file store, scheduler, provenance lineage, memoization, and C1–C8 contracts using a two-node constant/uppercase graph.
intention_for_embedding: To verify that the typed spine, hardened store, runtime scheduler, and all defined contracts compose and pass basic functional acceptance.

symbols.does: Updates a global ok flag and prints a PASS/FAIL line for a labeled boolean condition.
symbols.description: A lightweight assertion helper used throughout the acceptance script. It combines the supplied condition into the running ok flag and emits a human-readable pass/fail line, allowing the script to continue after a failure.

symbols.does: Sets up a temporary store, runs a two-node graph, and exercises store/scheduler/provenance/memo/contract checks before returning 0 or 1.
symbols.description: The acceptance entry point. It removes and recreates a temporary FsStore, builds a constant→uppercase graph, executes it via the scheduler, verifies provenance lineage and memoization, sanity-checks each C1–C8 contract, and prints the final E1 pass/fail result.
